rationale: The cited paper appears plausibly substantive because it provides a triadic governance perspective (buyer–vendor–supplier) that aligns with the citing paper’s stated move from dyadic to multiparty/network governance contexts. There is also strong methodological overlap: both notes describe a half-fraction 2^5 conjoint design analyzed with random-effects/hierarchical random-coefficients models. Given this conceptual and design similarity (plus exact DOI match), this is not an obvious mention-only citation.
